ar_score.label: Academic Reputation
ar_score.description: Based on a global survey of academics identifying the best universities in their field. Makes up 30% of the overall score.

er_score.label: Employer Reputation
er_score.description: Based on a global survey of graduate employers identifying universities producing the best graduates. Makes up 20% of the overall score.

fsr_score.label: Faculty Student Ratio
fsr_score.description: Measures teaching commitment by looking at the number of academic staff relative to enrolled students. Makes up 10% of the overall score.

cpp_score.label: Citations per Paper
cpp_score.description: Measures research impact by dividing the number of citations received by the number of papers produced. Makes up 10% of the overall score.

irn_score.label: International Research Network
irn_score.description: Measures a university's success in establishing and maintaining international research partnerships. Makes up 10% of the overall score.

ppf_score.label: Papers per Faculty
ppf_score.description: Measures research productivity by dividing the number of papers produced by the number of faculty members. Makes up 5% of the overall score.

swp_score.label: Staff with PhD
swp_score.description: Measures the proportion of academic staff who hold a doctoral degree. Makes up 5% of the overall score.

ifr_score.label: International Faculty Ratio
ifr_score.description: Measures the proportion of international faculty relative to total staff. Makes up 2.5% of the overall score.

isr_score.label: International Student Ratio
isr_score.description: Measures the ratio of international students to domestic students. Makes up 2.5% of the overall score.

inbound_score.label: Inbound Exchange Students
inbound_score.description: Measures the number of inbound exchange students relative to total student population. Makes up 2.5% of the overall score.

outbound_score.label: Outbound Exchange Students
outbound_score.description: Measures the number of outbound exchange students relative to total student population. Makes up 2.5% of the overall score.
